file_insights.purpose: Application class serving as entry point for dependency injection with Hilt

key_changes: Critical for Android runtime to instantiate custom Application class

patterns_discovered.description: Using @HiltAndroidApp annotation to enable compile-time DI code generation
patterns_discovered.significance: Establishes the foundation for DI throughout the application

patterns_discovered.description: Custom Application class with onCreate() override following Android conventions
patterns_discovered.significance: Ensures proper initialization at app startup

patterns_discovered.description: Application class placed in base package (com.inventory.app) with proper naming
patterns_discovered.significance: Follows Android naming conventions and organization

patterns_discovered.description: Application class explicitly declared in AndroidManifest.xml
patterns_discovered.significance: Required for Android runtime to recognize and use the custom Application

gotchas_discovered.description: The custom Application class MUST be registered in AndroidManifest.xml via android:name attribute, otherwise it won't be instantiated
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Properly added to manifest in this session

gotchas_discovered.description: @HiltAndroidApp must be on the Application class itself, not on Activities or other components
Must call super.onCreate() before any custom initialization to ensure proper lifecycle

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created InventoryApplication class with proper Hilt integration and manifest registration
key_achievements: Updated manifest to register custom Application

recommendations.details: Consider adding logging setup, crash reporting, or analytics initialization in onCreate() as the app grows
Could conditionally enable debugging or logging based on BuildConfig.DEBUG
As onCreate() is populated with code, document the order of initialization for future maintainers

recommendations.suggestion: Create unit tests for Application initialization
recommendations.details: Test that Application class initializes correctly with Hilt
